ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., agent, evaluator, investor, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date when the user account was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Date of the last activity */,
  "company" TEXT /* Name of the company the user works for */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the property data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the property data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the browser used */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in seconds */,
  "is_mobile" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access was from a mobile device */,
  "is_desktop" INTEGER /* Indicates if the access was from a desktop device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "renovations" (
  "renovation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each renovation */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property being renovated */,
  "renovation_date" TEXT /* Date of the renovation */,
  "renovation_type" TEXT /* Type of renovation (e.g., kitchen, bathroom, roofing) */,
  "renovation_cost" INTEGER /* Cost of the renovation */,
  "renovation_description" TEXT /* Description of the renovation */,
  "contractor_name" TEXT /* Name of the contractor who performed the renovation */,
  "contractor_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contractor */,
  "contractor_email" TEXT /* Email address of the contractor */,
  "is_warranty" INTEGER /* Indicates if the renovation is under warranty */,
  "warranty_expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the warranty */,
  "estimated_completion_date" TEXT /* Estimated date of completion for the renovation */,
  "actual_completion_date" TEXT /* Actual date of completion for the renovation */,
  "inspection_required" INTEGER /* Indicates if an inspection is required after the renovation */,
  "inspection_date" TEXT /* Date of the inspection after the renovation */,
  "inspection_result" TEXT /* Result of the inspection (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("renovation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "property_valuations" (
  "valuation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each valuation */,
  "property_id" INTEGER /* ID of the property being valued */,
  "valuation_date" TEXT /* Date of the valuation */,
  "valuation_type" TEXT /* Type of valuation (e.g., market, appraisal, prediction) */,
  "valuation_amount" INTEGER /* Amount of the valuation */,
  "valuation_method" TEXT /* Method used for valuation (e.g., comparable sales, machine learning) */,
  "valuation_agent" TEXT /* Name of the agent who performed the valuation */,
  "valuation_agent_company" TEXT /* Name of the company the valuation agent works for */,
  "valuation_agent_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the valuation agent */,
  "valuation_agent_email" TEXT /* Email address of the valuation agent */,
  "valuation_notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments on the valuation */,
  "valuation_accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the valuation (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "valuation_confidence" TEXT /* Confidence level of the valuation (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "valuation_source" TEXT /* Source of the valuation data (e.g., public records, private data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("valuation_id")
)
